scenarios.title: CI runs lint and tests on push
scenarios.gherkinSummary: Given a commit is pushed to main, When the CI workflow triggers, Then lint and test unit pass

scenarios.title: CI runs on pull requests
scenarios.gherkinSummary: Given a PR is opened against main, When the CI workflow triggers, Then lint + test + build run and PR shows pass/fail status

scenarios.title: Version tag produces versioned images
scenarios.gherkinSummary: Given a git tag v1.0.0 is pushed, When CI build completes, Then images are tagged smackerel-core:v1.0.0 and smackerel-ml:v1.0.0

scenarios.title: Untagged builds use commit SHA
scenarios.gherkinSummary: Given a commit without a tag is pushed, When CI build completes, Then images are tagged with the commit SHA

scenarios.title: ML image under 3GB
scenarios.gherkinSummary: Given the ML Dockerfile uses optimized multi-stage build, When the image is built, Then the final image is under 3GB and all runtime tests pass

scenarios.title: All config vars reach the smackerel-core container
scenarios.gherkinSummary: Given config/smackerel.yaml defines feature flags, When config generate + up runs, Then all variables from dev.env are available inside the container

scenarios.title: New config vars automatically flow to container
scenarios.gherkinSummary: Given a developer adds a new config key and updates config.sh, When config generate runs, Then the new var appears in dev.env and containers receive it without docker-compose.yml changes

scenarios.title: env_file replaces individual environment declarations
scenarios.gherkinSummary: Given docker-compose.yml uses env_file, When operator inspects it, Then there are no individual KEY: ${KEY} declarations for SST-managed vars

scenarios.title: Tagged release pushes images to GHCR
scenarios.gherkinSummary: Given a git tag v1.0.0 is pushed, When CI completes, Then smackerel-core:v1.0.0 and smackerel-ml:v1.0.0 are available at ghcr.io with OCI labels

scenarios.title: Operator deploys from pre-built images
scenarios.gherkinSummary: Given SMACKEREL_CORE_IMAGE is set to ghcr.io/owner/smackerel-core:v1.0.0, When operator runs ./smackerel.sh up, Then Compose pulls the pre-built image and services start

scenarios.title: Build-from-source remains the default
scenarios.gherkinSummary: Given no image override vars are set, When operator runs ./smackerel.sh build && ./smackerel.sh up, Then images are built from local Dockerfiles

scenarios.title: Documented branch protection settings cover required status checks for main
scenarios.gherkinSummary: Given the docs/Branch_Protection.md document exists, When an operator follows the documented settings for main, Then lint-and-test and build are required status checks that must pass before merge

scenarios.title: Branch protection requires PR review for solo developer workflow
scenarios.gherkinSummary: Given a solo developer wants to enable branch protection on main, When they follow the documented branch protection settings, Then required approving reviews defaults to 1 (optional for solo developer can set to 0)

scenarios.title: Go binary embeds version and commit hash via ldflags
scenarios.gherkinSummary: Given the Dockerfile passes VERSION and COMMIT_HASH as build args, When the Go binary is built with -ldflags -X main.version and -X main.commitHash, Then the running binary exposes version and commit values populated from ldflags

scenarios.title: Health endpoint response includes version commit hash and build time
scenarios.gherkinSummary: Given the core service is started with embedded version commitHash and buildTime, When GET /api/health is requested with admin auth, Then the response includes version commit hash and build time fields
